# Maddy Grubb Maps

> Freelance cartographer and GIS analyst based in Jackson, WY. Specializes in photorealistic 3D terrain maps, hand-drawn watercolor and pen & ink cartography, trail accessibility mapping, and scientific map design.

## About

Maddy Grubb is a freelance cartographer with a rare dual background in traditional fine art (watercolor, pen & ink) and scientific GIS analysis. She is one of a small number of cartographers in the US producing photorealistic 3D terrain maps using Blender and real GIS data. Her work spans national parks, ski resorts, conservation organizations, trail networks, private ranches, and fine art commissions.

## Services

- **Photorealistic 3D Maps** — terrain renders using Blender + GIS data; hyperrealistic mountain, canyon, and landscape maps
- **Trail & Outdoor Recreation Maps** — hiking trails, trailhead signage, kiosk maps, backcountry navigation, bikepacking and thru-hiking routes
- **Trail Accessibility Mapping** — ADA and PROWAG-compliant grade analysis and accessible trail maps for parks and trail organizations
- **Ski Resort & Mountain Operations Maps** — ski trail maps, heli-ski zones, backcountry ski area maps, mountain resort operations
- **National Park & Public Lands Maps** — visitor maps, science maps, interpretive maps for NPS and state parks
- **Climate & Conservation Maps** — glacier recession, species range, land use change, and environmental data visualization for nonprofits, scientists, and land trusts
- **Hand-Drawn Cartography** — watercolor maps, pen & ink illustrated maps, custom fine art map commissions
- **Ranch & Private Land Maps** — custom illustrated and GIS-based maps for private landowners, ranches, and estates
- **GIS Analysis & Science Communication** — spatial data processing, map design for research institutions and science publications

## Ideal Clients

National Park Service, state parks, trail associations, ski resorts, heli-ski operators, mountain biking networks, conservation nonprofits, land trusts, scientists and researchers, National Geographic, outdoor publications, private landowners and ranches, wealthy collectors commissioning fine art maps.

## Project Scale

Custom projects ranging from $5,000 to $100,000. Specializes in long-term and repeat client relationships.

## Location

Jackson, Wyoming, USA. Works with clients nationally and internationally.
